likert.text: I naturally think in terms of long-range goals and strategic plans.
I am energized by challenging the status quo to bring about necessary change.
I feel most satisfied when I have completed a practical task with excellence.
Building many positive, encouraging relationships is one of my highest priorities.
My primary drive is to create an environment where people feel emotionally safe and accepted.
I feel a deep need for quiet time alone to think, process, and recharge.
I am a good manager of resources (time, money, people) and use them wisely.
I am driven to build systems and structures that create order and ensure long-term success, and will see my plans come to fruition, even if it takes days, months, or years.
When I see a problem, my first instinct is to identify the root truth, even if it's uncomfortable.
People describe me as meticulous and conscientious in the tasks I complete for them.
I often verbally affirm others' potential and strengths in all spheres of my life.
I have a deep, intuitive sense of the emotional state of others and feel moved to undo their aloneness in places of pain.
Before I accept something as fact, I feel compelled to research and validate it for myself.
I get great joy, and am gifted at being hospitable and making people feel comfortable and cherished.
I am a natural leader and take a long-term view of leadership and the development of others.
I am more concerned with integrity and authenticity than with keeping everyone comfortable.
It frustrates me when I see people not doing their fair share of the work.
I am energized by rallying people together around a hopeful, positive vision.
I am known for my long-suffering unconditional love and acceptance.
I research deeper and more complex topics than most others just for the sake of knowing the information.
I usually have what others need, such as snacks, drinks, or other useful items, such as tissues, before others know that they need it.
I have goals in place and don't like to deviate from them until they are completed.
I am a pioneering spirit, always having new and innovative ideas and solutions.
People can count on me to be dependable and to finish the tasks I start.
I feel fulfilled when I can inspire and rally others to become passionate about what I am most passionate about at a given time.
I tend to deescalate conflict and am often a peacemaker.
I am frustrated by superficial conversations that don't interest me and prefer deep, intellectually stimulating discussions.
I am super resourceful.
I have little patience for activities that do not align with the main goal or vision.
I feel compelled to speak up against injustice or hypocrisy.
I show my love for others by taking practical action to meet their tangible needs.
I am naturally optimistic and believe the best about people and situations.
I can become emotionally overwhelmed because I sometimes absorb the pain of others.
I prefer to be alone studying the things I am passionate about or explaining them to interested individuals.
I enjoy stewarding and multiplying resources so I can be more generous.
My motto could be, "Good leaders create a vision, articulate the vision, passionately own the vision, and relentlessly drive it to completion." - Jack Welch.
I have a strong inner sense of what is right and wrong.
I often notice and move to meet the practical needs in a room before anyone else does.
I have a strong intuition about interpersonal dynamics and work to build bridges between people.
I am fiercely protective of the vulnerable and instinctively shield them from harm.
I am not easily swayed by peer pressure or others' approval, as I have strong convictions and desires.
I view the resources I have as something I've been entrusted with for the benefit of others.
I excel at architecting a long-term plan and then delegating to the right people to bring that vision to reality.
I am compelled to challenge the status quo and uproot broken systems to achieve radical, not just incremental, transformation.
I feel most fulfilled after a hard day of tangible, productive work.
I'm energized by rallying people together, using fun and celebration to build connections and inspire a shared sense of optimism.
I feel most loved when someone listens to me with their whole heart and understands my feelings.
I feel most loved when someone engages me in a deeply intellectual conversation.
I constantly am thinking about how myself and others can engage in wise financial stewardship.

forcedChoice.stem: I am more driven to...
forcedChoice.a: Challenge a broken system to get to the truth.
forcedChoice.b: Diligently complete the tasks needed to keep the current system running.

forcedChoice.stem: I am more driven to...
forcedChoice.a: Inspire a group of people with a hopeful, positive vision.
forcedChoice.b: Confront a group with an uncomfortable reality that leads to transformation.

forcedChoice.stem: I am more driven to...
forcedChoice.a: Speak a hard truth, even if it causes emotional discomfort.
forcedChoice.b: Protect someone's feelings, even if it means avoiding a hard truth.

forcedChoice.stem: When leading, my priority is...
forcedChoice.a: Ensuring the team stays the course on a well-thought-out master plan.
forcedChoice.b: Allowing for disruptive innovation that might improve upon the master plan.

forcedChoice.stem: I am more drawn to...
forcedChoice.a: A deep, analytical dive into a complex subject.
forcedChoice.b: A bold, pioneering adventure that challenges my limits.

forcedChoice.stem: My focus is more on...
forcedChoice.a: Wisely stewarding resources to fund a bold, risky, but potentially revolutionary idea or adventure.
forcedChoice.b: Wisely stewarding resources to ensure long-term stability and security.

forcedChoice.stem: I prefer to show support by...
forcedChoice.a: Cheerfully rallying and encouraging the person.
forcedChoice.b: Practically helping them with their to-do list.

forcedChoice.stem: I believe love is best expressed by...
forcedChoice.a: Providing tangible help and practical solutions.
forcedChoice.b: Providing a safe, empathetic presence for someone's emotions.

forcedChoice.stem: In a group project, I focus on...
forcedChoice.a: The long-term vision and overall strategy.
forcedChoice.b: The immediate tasks that need to be done right now.

forcedChoice.stem: I am more likely to be...
forcedChoice.a: Frustrated by inaction and analysis paralysis.
forcedChoice.b: Frustrated by rushed decisions made without thorough research.

forcedChoice.stem: When giving a gift, I am more concerned with...
forcedChoice.a: Ensuring everyone contributes fairly and the gift is practical.
forcedChoice.b: Ensuring the gift is generous, empowering, and makes the recipient feel cherished.

forcedChoice.stem: A healthy relationship requires...
forcedChoice.a: Deep emotional safety and unconditional acceptance.
forcedChoice.b: Uplifting joy and mutual celebration.

forcedChoice.stem: I am more inclined to...
forcedChoice.a: Let my decisions be guided by the emotional needs of the people involved.
forcedChoice.b: Let my decisions be guided by the objective goals of the plan.

forcedChoice.stem: My approach to problems is more...
forcedChoice.a: Analytical and logical, based on verifiable facts.
forcedChoice.b: Empathetic and intuitive, based on people's feelings.

forcedChoice.stem: When considering a large donation, I prioritize...
forcedChoice.a: The immediate emotional relief and comfort it will bring to an individual.
forcedChoice.b: The long-term strategic impact and stewardship of the organization receiving it.

forcedChoice.stem: I am more energized by...
forcedChoice.a: Creating a detailed, systematic plan to achieve a goal.
forcedChoice.b: Inspiring and rallying a team towards a shared objective.

forcedChoice.stem: I find it more important to be...
forcedChoice.a: Intellectually rigorous and precise.
forcedChoice.b: Relationally warm and encouraging.

forcedChoice.stem: When planning an event, I focus more on...
forcedChoice.a: The relational atmosphere and ensuring people have fun and connect.
forcedChoice.b: The budget and resources, ensuring a high-quality experience through wise stewardship.

forcedChoice.stem: My leadership style is more focused on...
forcedChoice.a: Architecting brilliant, long-term systems and plans.
forcedChoice.b: Patiently mentoring individuals to help them master a subject.

forcedChoice.stem: I would rather...
forcedChoice.a: Build a detailed budget and investment plan.
forcedChoice.b: Build a comprehensive research paper with extensive citations.

forcedChoice.stem: When making a big decision, I trust...
forcedChoice.a: A strategic plan that lays out the path to a future goal.
forcedChoice.b: A prudent financial plan that ensures resources are used wisely.

scenarios.stem: A volunteer project you are on is missing deadlines and team morale is plummeting.
options: Call an emergency meeting to re-architect the master plan, clarify the vision, and re-delegate tasks.
Privately check in with each team member to see how they are feeling, creating a safe space for them to voice concerns.
Find the biggest practical bottleneck and just start doing the work yourself to get momentum going again.
Directly confront the unspoken issue you believe is causing the failure, pushing for honesty to clear the air.

scenarios.stem: A close friend is devastated after being laid off from their job.
options: Immediately start reminding them of their strengths, telling them this is a setup for a comeback, and painting a positive picture of their future.
Offer a significant and thoughtful financial gift to cover their expenses for a month, empowering them to search for a new job without stress.
Spend hours with them researching the job market, analyzing their resume, and helping them build a logical, data-driven plan for their job search.
Sit with them in their grief, listen without judgment, and let them know you are there for them no matter what, validating their feelings of loss and fear.

scenarios.stem: You are on the planning committee for a large community-wide charity event.
options: Take charge of the budget and fundraising, ensuring every dollar is used wisely to create a high-quality, memorable, and generous experience.
Volunteer to be the MC or lead the welcoming committee, focusing on creating a fun, high-energy, and relational atmosphere.
Take on the role of overall event coordinator, developing the master timeline, defining roles for all teams, and making sure the event achieves its objective.
Sign up for the setup and teardown crew, quietly ensuring all the practical, hands-on work gets done with excellence.

scenarios.stem: You receive an unexpected financial windfall of $5,000.
options: Use it to fund a bold, adventurous trip, or invest it in a high-risk, high-reward idea you believe in.
Put it toward a major long-term goal (like a down payment or a business plan), allocating it according to your life strategy.
Immediately think of a person or cause that could benefit and give a generous, empowering gift.
Spend it on a fantastic party or group vacation to create a lasting, joyful memory with the people you love.
